## Signal Type: Documented-but-unused

**Signal: K8 — Weekly summary heartbeat (Sunday 23:55 UTC)**

**Signal strength:** MEDIUM — 3 consecutive windows of confirmed absence after eligible dates passed.

- skill.md P7 documents: if current time is Sunday 23:55 UTC ± 5min, compute weekly summary (jobs_total, jobs_success, jobs_failure, jobs_skipped, uptime_pct) and append to `logs/heartbeat.json`.
- Nakula was wired 2026-05-22. First eligible Sunday: 2026-05-24 23:55 UTC (IST 2026-05-25 05:25).
- Second eligible Sunday: 2026-05-31 23:55 UTC (not yet reached at time of this report).
- `logs/heartbeat.json` inspected in Windows 13 (Run 20), 14 (Run 21), and 15 (Run 22): contains only per-job sanjaya entries. No weekly-summary entry present.
- Absence after one eligible Sunday is weak evidence (could be timing). Absence is flagged here for proposal signal; the second eligible Sunday (2026-05-31) will confirm or clear.
- Supporting observations: Windows 13, 14, 15 = 3 windows confirming absence. Meets ≥3 threshold.

---

## Signal Type: Undocumented behavior

**Signal strength:** NONE qualifying at ≥3 observations.

nakula-run.sh (the cron wrapper) adds lockfile + bounded timeout + heartbeat-on-exit behavior. These properties are consistent with skill.md P3 (stale-lock cleanup), P6 (per-job lockfile), and P7 (heartbeat). No undocumented behaviors detected.

---

## Signal Type: Recurring failure

**Signal strength:** NONE. One missed cron run (2026-05-23 02:00 IST, crontab transition gap) documented in W21 but attributed to infrastructure wiring timing, not a nakula behavioral failure.

## Patterns Qualifying for Proposal

**QP-K8:** K8 (weekly summary heartbeat) is documented in skill.md P7 but has not been observed in heartbeat.json after the first eligible Sunday (2026-05-24). The proposal will flag this for Kartavya review: either (a) P7 is not yet implemented in nakula-run.sh and skill.md P7 should be annotated as aspirational/TODO, or (b) the implementation exists but there is a timing/trigger bug.

This is the only signal meeting the ≥3 qualifying threshold. All other skill.md procedures (K4, K5, K6, K9) are documented and not contradicted by any observation — they simply have not been exercised yet due to low invocation frequency.
